assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: '## Classification: Code Change Request' (line 9) and '**Action:** Sub-task created to address this feedback.' (line 17). subtask-30001.md exists with full sub-task content referencing comment 30001. report.md Review Feedback Classification table: '| 30001 | ... | Code change request | Sub-task created |' (line 31).

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: Suggestion' (line 9). Reasoning section (lines 13-20) explicitly identifies suggestive language: '"should also add" -- suggestive phrasing, proposing an addition rather than demanding a fix', '"would help" -- conditional/hedging language indicating this is a nice-to-have', '"Something like:" -- presents a possible implementation rather than a required one'. The reasoning explains these indicators clearly.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: '## Classification: Nit' (line 9) and '**Action:** No sub-task created.' (line 17). No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table: '| 30003 | ... | Nit | No sub-task |' (line 33).

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: '## Classification: Question' (line 9) and '**Action:** No sub-task created.' (line 22). No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md table: '| 30004 | ... | Question | No sub-task |' (line 34).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 3, value 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 9), '## Files to Modify' (line 12), '## Implementation Notes' (line 15), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 22). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md) and it has all required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 31-32: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. The URL matches exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md lines 34-38: '## Review Context' section containing '**Comment ID:** 30001', '**Author:** reviewer-a', '**File:** modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs, line 60', and '**Original comment:**' with the full original review comment text about wrapping soft_delete operations in a transaction.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/reporting files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md (classification outputs), subtask-30001.md (sub-task description), and report.md (verification report). No code files (.rs, .sql, .toml, etc.) exist in the outputs. The report and review files are all read-only analysis artifacts. No evidence of code modification in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md 'Review Feedback Classification' table (lines 29-34) lists all four comments with their classifications and actions. The report's 'Detailed Analysis' section (lines 36-42) provides detailed analysis of all four comments.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The overall result is 'WARN' (line 19) with a note that 'One code change request from reviewer feedback requires attention'. The report only analyzes and reports findings; it does not take any merge action.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 16: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | `tests/api/sbom_delete.rs` is a new file with 5 new test functions; no modified or deleted test files |'. Also in the detailed section lines 139-142: '#### Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE' with explanation 'tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file (not present on the base branch). It adds 5 new test functions... Classification: ADDITIVE -- only new test coverage added, no existing tests changed or removed.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md lines 20-21: 'Additionally, no project convention in the fixture data backs upgrading this to a code change request. The repository's CONVENTIONS.md is referenced in repo-backend.md but its contents are not provided in the fixtures, meaning there is no documented convention about index creation patterns... Without concrete CONVENTIONS.md evidence or a demonstrated codebase pattern of creating indexes on nullable filter columns, this remains classified as a suggestion.' report.md lines 117-118 (Style/Conventions > Convention Upgrade): 'One suggestion was evaluated for convention upgrade (comment 30002 -- index on deleted_at). No CONVENTIONS.md content was available in the fixtures to match against, and no codebase pattern evidence was available... The suggestion was not upgraded; it remains classified as a suggestion.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: '## Classification: Suggestion' (line 9) and '**Action:** No sub-task created.' (line 22). No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory (only subtask-30001.md exists). report.md table line 32: '| 30002 | ... | Suggestion | No sub-task |'. The reasoning in review-30002.md explicitly states no convention supports upgrading: 'no project convention in the fixture data backs upgrading this to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 15: '| Test Quality | PASS | No repetitive tests detected; all test functions have doc comments; Eval Quality: N/A |'. Lines 136-137 (detailed section): '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' followed by 'No eval result reviews found on the PR. No `github-actions[bot]` reviews with `## Eval Results` marker detected.' The report explicitly marks Eval Quality as N/A and states the detection criteria found no matches.
